it SideBar Main / MbedGcc Main.MbedGcc History Hide minor edits - Show changes to output August 11, 2016, at 09:11 pm by 11douglash - Added instructions on how to setup offline mbed compiling Added lines 1-11: Use the following instructions to setup

nes 1-11: Use the following instructions to setup a machine to compile an mbed binary offline, using GCC arm: The base code can be cloned from a git repo, or you can export an online mbed compiler to "GCC Arm" to run these instructions. * sudo add-ap

"GCC Arm" to run these instructions. * sudo add-apt-repository ppa:terry.guo/gcc-arm-embedded * sudo apt-get install gcc-arm-none-eabi Then, you can run the command "make" to successfully compile the code. You must edit the makefile every time a new

code. You must edit the makefile every time a new file is added, so that it compiles&links that file. A good example of a makefile modified in such a way is [[https://github.com/ucb-ee106/ee106_zumy_support/blob/master/zumy_mbed/Makefile|this]] one.
